title: HTB HTB Proxy — URL Encoding Bypass + Command Injection
excerpt: A Go reverse proxy blocks access to a vulnerable Node.js route by matching the lowercased URL. URL-encoding one character bypasses the filter while Express still routes the request, enabling OS command injection via an unsanitized ip wrapper.

title: HTB Jailbreak — XXE via Firmware Update XML
excerpt: The firmware update endpoint parses XML without disabling external entities. Injecting a SYSTEM entity pointing to /flag.txt and fitting it inside the expected FirmwareUpdateConfig schema leaks the file contents.

title: HTB Flag Command — Hidden Secret Command via JS Debug
excerpt: The game's CheckMessage function accepts commands from a secret key in the availableOptions object. The key's value is fully exposed in client-side JavaScript — find it with DevTools and send it to get the flag.

title: CVE-2026-33890 — Unauthenticated Admin Privilege Escalation via Passkey Registration
excerpt: MyTube's WebAuthn passkey registration endpoints are exposed without authentication. An attacker can register an arbitrary passkey and receive a full admin token — zero credentials required.

title: CVE-2026-33735 — Improper Access Control Allows Complete Application Takeover
excerpt: A flawed string-matching access control check in MyTube's middleware can be bypassed by appending a query parameter, allowing a visitor to fully replace the application's SQLite database.

title: CVE-2026-33935 — Unauthenticated Account Lockout via Shared Login Attempt State
excerpt: All three of MyTube's password verification endpoints share a single global login attempt counter. Any unauthenticated attacker can trigger a rolling 24-hour DoS against all password-based auth.

title: HTB Getting Started — Stack Overwrite
excerpt: An intro pwn challenge that visualizes the stack for you. Calculate the 40-byte offset from the buffer to the deadbeef target and overwrite it with 0x1337babe.

title: HTB Regularity — Shellcode Injection via jmp rsi
excerpt: A no-libc assembly binary with a buffer overflow in a custom read function. RSI points to our buffer on return, so we overwrite the return address with a jmp rsi gadget and land in shellcode.

title: HTB Labyrinth — Ret2Win with Stack Alignment
excerpt: A classic ret2win: enter the correct door to trigger a buffer overflow, overwrite the return address to escape_plan, and align the stack with a ret gadget to capture the flag.

title: HTB APKey — Android APK RE with Frida Hooking
excerpt: An obfuscated Android APK hides a decryption routine behind a hashed-password login. Patching the target SDK with apktool to install the app, then using a two-line Frida script to call the decryption function directly bypasses the check entirely.

title: HTB BinCrypt Breaker — XOR Decrypt and Reverse the Hidden Binary
excerpt: A checker binary XORs file.bin with 0xab to produce a valid ELF, but its open() call is broken by design. Replicating the XOR in Python yields the hidden binary, whose flag validator is reversed by undoing 8 rounds of character shuffling and targeted XOR operations.

title: HTB Coffee Invocation — JNI Dynamic Class Loading with Obfuscated Verification
excerpt: A native binary dynamically loads two Java classes via JNI and runs them against the input. The first class compares characters through tampered byte and short conversion tables; reversing both tables recovers the expected input characters.

title: HTB VirtuallyMad — Custom VM Reverse Engineering
excerpt: A binary implements a tiny VM with MOV, ADD, and SUB opcodes over four registers. The win condition requires specific register values achieved in exactly 5 instructions — craft the opcode string and the VM prints the flag.

title: HTB Wayback — Timestamp-Seeded RNG Brute Force
excerpt: A C++ password generator seeds srand() with a formula derived from the current date and time. Knowing the approximate generation date, we replicate the seed formula in Python via ctypes, brute-force all second-level timestamps, and decrypt the AES-CBC flag.

title: BSidesTLV 2025 — AvengersQueryLanguage: GraphQL Introspection to Hidden Type
excerpt: The app queries a GraphQL API exposed at /graphql. Running introspection reveals a ClassifiedReport type not linked from the frontend. Querying it directly leaks the flag from its content field.

title: BSidesTLV 2025 — BruteHush: Character-by-Character Password Brute Force
excerpt: The binary leaks a distinct response when our input is a correct prefix of the stored password. A character-by-character brute force over printable ASCII recovers the full password one position at a time.

title: BSidesTLV 2025 — ComicsMCP: SSRF via MCP Universe Parameter
excerpt: An MCP tool constructs outbound URLs from user input as bstlv25-{universe}.chals.io/{name}. Injecting @ into the universe parameter redirects the request to an attacker-controlled host, leaking the flag in the HTTP headers.

title: BSidesTLV 2025 — shortcut: bcrypt 72-Byte Truncation Bypass
excerpt: bcrypt silently truncates input beyond 72 bytes. Sending api_version as a JSON array of one long string passes the len() == 1 check, but str() of the list is long enough to fill bcrypt's entire 72-byte budget, making expected and received hashes identical regardless of credentials.

title: BSidesTLV 2025 — Signature Studio: ECDSA Signature Malleability
excerpt: The server refuses to reuse a signature, but ECDSA malleability means (r, s) and (r, n-s) are both valid signatures for the same message. Negate s to produce a second valid signature the server has never seen and collect the flag.
